name: Artie Shaw
abstract: Artie Shaw (born Arthur Jacob Arshawsky; May 23, 1910 – December 30, 2004) was an American clarinetist, composer, bandleader, and author of both fiction and non-fiction. Widely regarded as "one of jazz's finest clarinetists," Shaw led one of the United States' most popular big bands in the late 1930s through the early 1940s.

interviews.title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Paul Whiteman and His All American Band

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: I went with the Artie Shaw band in 1940. And now from ‘40 through ‘45 I think I played with three different Artie Shaw bands, and wrote arrangements and played trombone
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: all six of us joined Artie Shaw... And so we went with him... I stayed with Artie until he came to New York. I did a lot of recording with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: And then I heard Artie Shaw, and Artie Shaw really was… Of course, Artie Shaw became my favorite for a long, he still is one of my favorites.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Galloway
about_quotes.evidence: I remember his name, he was on late every night out of Frankfurt, his name was Joe Neidig. His theme song was Artie Shaw’s “Nightmare,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Galloway interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: And he actually introduced Artie Shaw to Buddy Rich... And he said to Artie Shaw, “Oh you want to hire this guy, he’s really great.”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: My family was musical. In fact they told me that two weeks before I was born they got front row seats to a concert by Artie Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013
